candidateContexts.label: admin basic-info CRUD administration surface (contacts, stations, trains, configs, prices, forwarded to their owning services)
admin basic-info price administration surface (PriceInfo CRUD forwarded to ts-price-service)
admin order administration surface, branch-selecting ts-order-service vs ts-order-other-service by trainNumber prefix
admin route administration surface, validating station membership then forwarding to ts-route-service / ts-station-service

candidateCovers.label: shared JWT-filter plus role-gate plus permissive-CORS authority configuration pattern across the admin-* backoffice services

notes: Spring Boot entrypoint, registers a load-balanced RestTemplate bean, no domain logic.
Five specific GET reads under adminbasic are opened without authentication while the rest of the adminbasic path tree requires ADMIN role.
Global CORS bean allows all origins/methods/headers with credentials disabled.
Thin REST controller, every handler logs then delegates directly to AdminBasicInfoService with no local validation.
Lombok DTO; price rate fields are declared as double primitives with no string parsing observed in this file.
Pure declaring interface, javadoc only, no bodies; behavioral evidence lives in the Impl class instead.
Every method unconditionally forwards to exactly one downstream owning service (contacts/station/train/config/price); DiscoveryClient is injected but never invoked in these bodies.
No status-code branching observed here (unlike the order and route service impls).
Registers with Nacos discovery on port 18767, swagger controller package adminbasic.microservice.
Spring Boot entrypoint identical in shape to the other admin-* application classes.
Unlike admin-basic-info, the whole adminorderservice path tree requires ADMIN role with no unauthenticated GET carve-outs.
Only getAllOrders carries @CrossOrigin among the five endpoints; add/update/delete do not.
Declaring interface only, no method bodies.

deleteOrder/updateOrder/addOrder all branch on the same trainNumber-starts-with-G-or-D condition to pick ts-order-service vs ts-order-other-service; getAllOrders instead calls both unconditionally and merges results.
Registers with Nacos discovery on port 16112.
Spring Boot entrypoint identical in shape to the other admin-* application classes.
Same shape as the order-service SecurityConfig: whole adminrouteservice path tree requires ADMIN role.
The addRoute endpoint method delegates to a differently-named service method (createAndModifyRoute).
Declaring interface only, no method bodies.
createAndModifyRoute first checks that start/end stations are members of the request's station list locally, then re-validates existence against ts-station-service before calling ts-route-service.
Downstream HTTP status is compared against HttpStatus.ACCEPTED (202) at three call sites; anything else only logs an error but the body is still returned.
Registers with Nacos discovery on port 16113.
Spring Boot entrypoint only; no controller, service, security, or resource files for ts-admin-travel-service fall in this chunk.
